name: Razorpay Checkout — Saga Pattern
scenario: Razorpay's checkout flow spans 4 microservices. A bug in NotificationService is leaving payments charged with no order created. Implement an orchestration Saga with compensating transactions.

core.description: SagaStep enum (INVENTORY_RESERVED, ORDER_CREATED, PAYMENT_CHARGED, NOTIFICATION_SENT) and SagaState class: mark(step), is(step), getCompletedSteps(). LinkedHashSet for ordering.
CheckoutSagaOrchestrator @Service: 4 steps with compensation in REVERSE order on any failure. Catches all exceptions from any step.
Idempotent compensations: ReleaseInventory, CancelOrder, RefundPayment — all check Set<String> before acting (ALREADY_DONE vs DONE result).
SagaLog JPA entity + repository: persist STARTED before step, COMPLETED after, COMPENSATED on undo. Crash recovery reads log to find last known state.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): success=all 4 steps, step2 fail=inventory released, step3 fail=order+inventory compensated, double-compensate=idempotent. All 4 pass.
README: explains why compensations must be idempotent and in reverse order.

bonus.description: SagaStateMachine: Map<SagaStatus,Set<SagaStatus>> allowed transitions. Transition throws IllegalStateException for invalid moves. Tests: valid path, skip-step throws.
Choreography alternative: Spring ApplicationEvents replacing the orchestrator. OrderCreatedEvent → InventoryService reserves → PaymentFailedEvent → all services compensate.
Micrometer: saga.completed counter (outcome tag), saga.duration timer, saga.compensation.triggered counter (step tag). Test: metrics recorded correctly.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Compensate only COMPLETED steps: use state.is(step) before each compensation — don't compensate a step that never ran. Compensation in REVERSE order: if steps 1,2,3 ran and step 3 failed, compensate 2 then 1 (never 1 then 2 — dependencies). Idempotency: check Set<String> processedIds before acting; return ALREADY_DONE if already compensated. SagaLog: save STARTED BEFORE executing the step (if crash during step, log shows step was started). 2PC alternative: impossible for cross-service sagas — payment gateways don't support distributed locks.
